metadata.head_at_handoff_after_this_commit: (this commit -- one parent ahead of 3b9b1b8; the v3 handoff XML is the only file in the diff)
metadata.note: This file SUPPLEMENTS docs/platform_separation/ea_outputs/EA_HANDOFF_STAGE2_MIDSTAGE.xml (v1, commit 17086a8) and docs/platform_separation/ea_outputs/EA_HANDOFF_STAGE2_MIDSTAGE_v2.xml (commit 3b9b1b8). All three stay on disk for audit history. v3 records the proactive handoff issued by Stage-2 EA Instance 3 after applying the canonical refactor pattern to 6 of 13 Item-2.4 entrypoint files, before context exhaustion would have forced an emergency stop. Per binding ack i1-4 in 2.5-EA_ACKNOWLEDGMENTS_PROCEED_TO_ITEM_2.4.xml, this is a docs-only commit; the 6-file refactor work remains UNCOMMITTED in the working tree for the successor.
metadata.stop_reason: proactive_handoff_pre_context_exhaustion (predecessor-self-directed; canonical refactor pattern proven across 6 files of varying complexity, remaining 7 include MCP server which is the largest single lift)

ground_truth_at_handoff.git_status_porcelain: M pyproject.toml M tools/autonomy_budget/config.yaml M tools/autonomy_budget/config_loader.py M tools/autonomy_budget/tests/test_config_loader.py M tools/fleet_observability/credential_rotation_reminder.py M tools/fleet_observability/daily_digest.py M tools/fleet_observability/dashboard_maintainer.py M tools/fleet_observability/weekly_summary.py M tools/fleet_observability/welcome_back_digest.py M tools/gate_stale_cleaner/run_live.py ?? "docs/platform_separation/Temp for responses/2.4-GUIDE_RESPONSE_ITERATION_2_APPROVED_03_STAGE2_REFACTOR_MULTIPROJECT.xml" ?? "docs/platform_separation/Temp for responses/2.4-GUIDE_RESPONSE_TO_EA_INITIALIZATION_03_STAGE2_REFACTOR_MULTIPROJECT.xml" ?? "docs/platform_separation/Temp for responses/2.4-RESPONSE-EA_COMPREHENSION_GATE_ITERATION_2_STAGE2.xml" ?? "docs/platform_separation/Temp for responses/2.5-EA_ACKNOWLEDGMENTS_PROCEED_TO_ITEM_2.4.xml" ?? tools/_project_context.py ?? tools/_vikunja_client.py ?? tools/autonomy_budget/projects/ ?? tools/tests/
ground_truth_at_handoff.git_log_oneline_3: 3b9b1b8 (HEAD -> chore/platform-extraction) docs(platform_separation): Stage 2 mid-stage EA handoff v2 -- items 2.1+2.2+2.3 complete (uncommitted), resume at 2.4 17086a8 docs(platform_separation): Stage 2 mid-stage EA handoff -- items 2.1+2.2 complete (uncommitted), resume at 2.3 ef0e30e docs(platform_separation): Stage 1→2 handoffs (Guide instance 2→3 + EA Stage 1→Stage 2)
ground_truth_at_handoff.stash_zero_untouched: stash@{0}: On main: wake_launcher auto-stash sdo 20260424_013002 -- DO NOT TOUCH
ground_truth_at_handoff.note: v3 inherits all v2 ground-truth invariants. The git_status_porcelain delta vs v2 is exactly the 6 refactored files (M tools/fleet_observability/credential_rotation_reminder.py + 5 others; see work_completed/item[@id='2.4_partial']).

work_completed_inherited.item: Per-project allowlist overlay seam (config_loader.py + projects/blarai.yaml + 6 overlay tests).
work_completed_inherited.pyproject_change: tools/tests added to testpaths (additive, defaults preserved).
work_completed_inherited.note: Refer to v1 (commit 17086a8) and v2 (commit 3b9b1b8) for the verbatim file lists, test names, and diff content. v3 does not duplicate.

item.name: argparse + ProjectContext refactor of main()-bearing entrypoints (6 of 13)
item.canonical_pattern_proven: Six files now share the same template. Successor MUST apply the same template to remaining files. The pattern is byte-identical to today's behavior when no flags are passed AND ctx resolves to BlarAI default (verified by walking _project_context.resolve(None, None) for cwd-inside-BlarAI: returns ProjectContext(root=BlarAI absolute, name="BlarAI", vikunja_project_id=3); then ctx.name.lower() == "blarai" matches existing tools/autonomy_budget/projects/blarai.yaml overlay file).
item.pattern_template:
# At top of file: import argparse from tools import _project_context from tools.autonomy_budget import config_loader
def _build_parser() -> argparse.ArgumentParser: parser = argparse.ArgumentParser(description="...") parser.add_argument( "--project-root", type=str, default=None, help="Project root directory (defaults to BlarAI).", ) parser.add_argument( "--project-id", type=int, default=None, help="Vikunja project_id of the project (sanity-checked vs registry).", ) return parser
def main(argv: list[str] | None = None) -> int: args = _build_parser().parse_args(argv) ctx = _project_context.resolve(args.project_root, args.project_id) cfg = config_loader.load_config( path=ctx.root / "tools" / "autonomy_budget" / "config.yaml", project_overlay_path=ctx.root / "tools" / "autonomy_budget" / "projects" / f"{ctx.name.lower()}.yaml", ) # ... rest unchanged. For helpers that previously called load_config() # themselves, accept ctx kwarg with `None` default that auto-resolves # for backwards compat. For helpers that used module-level REPO_ROOT, # accept repo_root kwarg with `REPO_ROOT` default; main() passes ctx.root.

file.change: Added imports: argparse, _project_context. Added _build_parser() helper. Changed `def main() -> int:` to `def main(argv: list[str] | None = None) -> int:` with ctx resolution and explicit config paths.
file.body_unchanged: LiveVikunjaClient instantiation, health_check, create_task with cfg["vikunja"]["infrastructure_project_id"] all unchanged.

file.change: Same pattern as credential_rotation_reminder.py. Added _build_parser() with description="Welcome-back digest producer (M1 per DEC-11 §3.3).". Refactored main() signature + body intro. Body (state.read_state, last_la_action_utc check, transition detection, create_task) UNCHANGED.
More complex due to module-level REPO_ROOT and a build_digest() helper that previously called load_config() itself.

details.d: build_digest() now takes `*, ctx: "_project_context.ProjectContext | None" = None` kwarg; if None, calls _project_context.resolve(); calls load_config() with explicit paths derived from ctx.root; passes ctx.root to _scan_logs_for_runs (path arg) and _git_log_oneline_since (repo_root arg).
main() argparse + ctx resolve + explicit load_config paths, then `body = build_digest(client, ctx=ctx)`.
Note: dashboard_maintainer.py imports _git_log_oneline_since from this module; the new repo_root kwarg defaults to REPO_ROOT so import-side callers are unaffected.

file.change: Same pattern as daily_digest.py (REPO_ROOT preserved as default; build_summary takes ctx kwarg; _git_log_stats takes repo_root kwarg with REPO_ROOT default).
Same simple pattern as credential_rotation_reminder.py (no module-level REPO_ROOT to handle; no helper that calls load_config).
file.known_open_issue: This file calls `live._request("POST", f"/tasks/{...}", json={...})` (private method). That is Item 2.6 chokepoint work, NOT 2.4. Successor: leave for 2.6.

item.compile_validation: get_errors run on all 6 files: NO errors found in any.

item.name: argparse + ProjectContext refactor of remaining 7 files

file.approach: NO argparse (library, called by agents in-process). Add `project_context: "_project_context.ProjectContext | None" = None` kwarg to `run()`. When provided AND `config_path is None`, derive config_path = `ctx.root / "tools" / "autonomy_budget" / "config.yaml"`. Existing `config_path` / `state_path` / `lifts_path` kwargs remain (used by 5 tests in tools/autonomy_budget/tests/test_self_check.py). Existing test invocations use explicit paths and are unaffected.
file.load_config_call_site: line 76: `cfg = config_loader.load_config(config_path)` — keep positional for backwards compat; if config_path is None and project_context is provided, substitute the derived path before calling.
file.note: Guide-3 ack listed self_check as part of the canonical 12. Library-style refactor (not CLI).

file.approach: Likely NO change required. cleaner.run_sweep(client, config) takes both injected; ctx threading flows via the caller (run_live.py main, already refactored). VERIFY by reading the file: confirm no module-level REPO_ROOT, no hardcoded BlarAI paths, no direct load_config calls.

This is the LEGACY pure-HTTP client. Item 2.6 routes call sites through the new tools/_vikunja_client.VikunjaClient chokepoint with _require_project_id guard. For Item 2.4, NO ctx-related change (HTTP client has no project knowledge). Re-confirm at Item 2.6 whether the legacy client gets folded entirely or kept as a thin shim.

Constructor signature line 92: `repo_root: Path | str = Path(r"C:/Users/mrbla/BlarAI")`. Replace hardcoded default with `_project_context.DEFAULT_BLARAI_ROOT` (or sentinel + lazy resolve at construction time). Also add `vikunja_project_id: int | None = None` kwarg if needed for cross-project parity (the class hardcodes `PROJECT_BLARAI_CORE_DEVELOPMENT: int = 3` at module scope — Item 2.6 will likely move that to ctx.vikunja_project_id).

Already takes repo_root explicitly per existing tests (tools/fleet_ops/tests/test_diff_builder.py uses `repo_root=tmp_path`). Likely NO change. VERIFY by reading the file: confirm no module-level hardcoded BlarAI paths.
Need to read. If module-level REPO_ROOT exists, replace with function-level resolution. If functions take repo_root explicitly already, no change.

This is the BIGGEST single refactor in Stage 2. The MCP server runs as a long-lived process invoked via `if __name__ == "__main__":` at line 662. Approach: 1. Resolve ProjectContext at startup (NO CLI flags — MCP servers are spawned by Claude Desktop with no argv control). Use env-based: `ctx = _project_context.resolve()` (which honors BLARAI_PROJECT_ROOT env var). 2. Refactor handlers to route through `tools._vikunja_client.VikunjaClient` with `_require_project_id` guard. 3. CRITICAL FIX (Master Plan §D.1 violation): `list_tasks(project_id: int | None = None)` MUST become `list_tasks(*, project_id: int)` — the None default leaks BlarAI scope across project boundaries. Same for `search_tasks` and any other handler with project_id default. 4. Verify all 19 MCP handlers route through the chokepoint. The 19 handlers per CLAUDE.md: list_projects, create_project, get_project, update_project, delete_project, list_tasks, create_task, get_task, update_task, complete_task, delete_task, list_labels, create_label, add_label_to_task, list_task_comments, add_task_comment, search_tasks, bulk_create_tasks, project_summary.
file.warning: This single file may consume an entire successor session by itself. Successor SHOULD plan to handle MCP server + items 2.5/2.6 in one focused session, possibly emitting v4 handoff before items 2.7-2.10.

item.name: Verify CLI surface on all modified entrypoints
item.verification: For each main()-bearing file in canonical 12: run `python -m --help` from BlarAI cwd and confirm both `--project-root` and `--project-id` flags appear. For MCP server: spawn it and verify handlers route through chokepoint by inspecting log lines or mocking the underlying VikunjaClient.

item.name: PowerShell scripts: -BlarAIRoot param + critical_notify.ps1 inspection
item.files_in_scope: 7 PS1 scripts in tools/scheduled-tasks/ — successor must enumerate via `Get-ChildItem -Recurse tools/*.ps1 tools/scheduled-tasks/*.ps1`. Each gets `[Parameter(Mandatory=$false)] [string] $BlarAIRoot = "C:\Users\mrbla\BlarAI"` added; usages of hardcoded `C:\Users\mrbla\BlarAI` get replaced with `$BlarAIRoot`.
item.special_attention: tools/scheduled-tasks/critical_notify.ps1 — high-criticality alerting; preserve exact email/Slack payloads byte-for-byte.

work_remaining.item: After 2.5: dot-source each PS1 with no args and confirm $BlarAIRoot defaults to BlarAI; with `-BlarAIRoot 'C:\test'` confirm override propagates.

item.name: Vikunja chokepoint extended grep + routing
item.verification: `grep -r "https?://localhost:3456" tools/ --include="*.py"` → all hits MUST be inside tools/_vikunja_client.VikunjaClient or its tests. Any other call site is a chokepoint violation; route it through the chokepoint. Same for `requests.get/post` patterns hitting the Vikunja API.
item.legacy_disposition: Decide final disposition of tools/fleet_observability/_vikunja_client.LiveVikunjaClient: (a) delegate all methods through tools/_vikunja_client.VikunjaClient (thin shim), or (b) fold entirely (delete LiveVikunjaClient, update ~6 callers to instantiate VikunjaClient directly). Recommendation: (a) thin shim for now to keep the diff bounded; full fold in Stage 5 cleanup.

item.name: Test gate -- pytest ≥280 + V1-V7 verifications
item.baseline: ≥280 passing (pre-Stage-2 baseline carried from v2). Stage 2 should NET-ADD tests: V8 defaults preservation tests, V1-V7 V matrix tests per Master Plan §V.
item.V_matrix: V1=ProjectContext resolution (covered by 9 tests in test_project_context.py); V2=VikunjaClient scope guard (covered by 6 tests in test_vikunja_client_scope.py); V3=overlay merge (covered by 6 tests in test_config_loader.py); V4=CLI flag exposure (NEW; one parametrized test per refactored entrypoint); V5=PS1 -BlarAIRoot (NEW; pester or pytest-via-subprocess); V6=MCP server project_id rejection (NEW; mock-based); V7=cross-project byte-identity (NEW; run with two project overlays and diff outputs).

work_remaining.item: After 2.7: enumerate test count delta (v3 → final); confirm V1-V7 each have at least one passing test.

item.name: $BLARAI_PID=3 hard gate preflight
item.command: python -c "from tools import _project_context; ctx = _project_context.resolve(); assert ctx.vikunja_project_id == 3, f'expected 3, got {ctx.vikunja_project_id}'; print('OK ctx.vikunja_project_id =', ctx.vikunja_project_id)"
item.on_drift: STOP. Do NOT proceed to commit. Report to LA: registry .platform/vikunja_project_ids.yaml has wrong value or resolution chain is broken.

item.name: SINGLE Stage-2 commit
item.scope: One commit folding ALL Stage-2 work: items 2.1+2.2+2.3 (currently uncommitted from v1/v2 session) + items 2.4+2.5+2.6+2.7 (uncommitted from successor sessions) + Item 2.8 preflight evidence. Pyproject.toml MUST be in this commit (per binding ack i1-3).
item.commit_template:
chore(platform_separation): Stage 2 multi-project refactor (DEC-15 platform extraction)
Items 2.1-2.8 of 03_STAGE2_REFACTOR_MULTIPROJECT.xml.
Adds: - tools/_vikunja_client.VikunjaClient with _require_project_id guard (chokepoint) - tools/_project_context.ProjectContext + 5-step resolver - tools/autonomy_budget/projects/blarai.yaml overlay - --project-root / --project-id flags on all <N> Python entrypoints - -BlarAIRoot param on 7 PS1 helpers - V1-V7 verification matrix (tests/)
Refactors: - <N> Python entrypoints to thread ProjectContext - All Vikunja API call sites through VikunjaClient chokepoint - MCP server handlers to require explicit project_id (Master Plan §D.1)
Defaults preserved byte-for-byte for the BlarAI-only invocation: - cwd-inside-BlarAI + no flags + no env -> ctx.vikunja_project_id == 3 (V8) - All test invocations and scheduled-task XMLs unchanged
Test baseline: <X> passing (pre: 280; net add: <delta>).
Fleet stays paused; no merge to main; stash@{0} untouched.
item.forbidden: NO partial commits. NO emergency single-commit attempts that omit pending V matrix tests or any item 2.4-2.7 file.

item.name: Append-only STATUS.md update
item.approach: Append a Stage 2 closure entry to docs/platform_separation/STATUS.md (do not rewrite existing entries). Reference the Stage-2 commit hash from 2.9, list the 13 entrypoints + 7 PS1 + MCP server + V matrix tests, declare Stage 2 CLOSED.

standing_rules_carried_forward.rule: Fleet stays paused throughout (verify state.json on each substantive edit; never call state.resume_fleet).
No merge to main until LA explicitly approves; the chore/platform-extraction branch stays alive.
stash@{0} (wake_launcher auto-stash sdo 20260424_013002) is UNTOUCHED — DO NOT git stash pop or git stash drop.
$BLARAI_PID preflight at 2.8 MUST resolve to exactly 3. STOP and report to LA on any drift.
SINGLE Stage-2 commit at 2.9 only. Until then, all refactor work stays uncommitted in the working tree (auto-stash hazard does NOT apply because fleet is paused).
Per binding ack i1-4: if successor approaches context exhaustion before 2.9, emit v4 mid-stage handoff XML following same schema, commit ONLY that file, stop. NO partial-commit refactor work.

Successor MUST start with comprehension gate per copilot-instructions.md §Execution_Agent. Read v1 + v2 + v3 + 2.4-GUIDE_RESPONSE_ITERATION_2_APPROVED_03_STAGE2_REFACTOR_MULTIPROJECT.xml + 2.5-EA_ACKNOWLEDGMENTS_PROCEED_TO_ITEM_2.4.xml + 03_STAGE2_REFACTOR_MULTIPROJECT.xml; then present comprehension summary to LA and wait for approval.

successor_immediate_next_actions.step: Read this v3 + v2 + v1 fully. Read iteration-2 work plan (2.4-RESPONSE-EA_COMPREHENSION_GATE_ITERATION_2_STAGE2.xml lines 60-200) to recover the canonical 13 file list. Read the 6 already-refactored files (credential_rotation_reminder, welcome_back_digest, daily_digest, weekly_summary, dashboard_maintainer, run_live) end-to-end to internalize the proven pattern.

Verify ground truth matches: `git status --porcelain` should show the 10 M files + 4 ?? Temp files + 4 ?? new tools artifacts listed in this v3's ground_truth_at_handoff. `git log --oneline -1` should show the v3 handoff commit (one ahead of 3b9b1b8). Fleet still paused. stash@{0} untouched.
Present comprehension gate to LA. Wait for approval.

After approval, refactor the 7 remaining Item-2.4 files in this order: (a) self_check.py [smallest library lift], (b) blarai_next_task_resolver.py [single line default change + module constant decision], (c) cleaner.py + diff_builder.py + active_tasks.py [verify-only or single-line changes], (d) _vikunja_client.py legacy [no change for 2.4; defer disposition to 2.6], (e) vikunja_mcp/server.py [LARGEST — may need its own focused session].
After all 13 entrypoint-side files complete + compile clean: proceed to 2.4.v2 verification.
Then items 2.5 → 2.10 in order. Emit v4 handoff if context tightens before 2.9.

handoff_metadata_for_audit.session_token_budget_consumed_at_emissio: ~80% (estimate based on conversation length: prior compaction summary + 13-file audit re-load + 6-file pattern application + multi_replace_string_in_file batch + this v3 authoring)
handoff_metadata_for_audit.pattern_validation_evidence: get_errors on all 6 refactored files: zero errors. Pattern proven on simplest case (credential_rotation_reminder, no helpers) AND most complex case (daily_digest with module REPO_ROOT + build_digest helper that calls load_config).

decision_log_for_successor.d: For helpers that previously called config_loader.load_config() themselves (build_digest, build_summary): chose to make them accept `*, ctx: ProjectContext | None = None` kwarg with auto-resolve fallback. Alternative considered: require ctx (no default) and force callers to pass it. Rejected because in-process callers (e.g., test code) would break. The current design is backwards-compat-preserving while still allowing main() to thread the explicit ctx.

For helpers that used module-level REPO_ROOT (_git_log_oneline_since, _git_log_stats): chose to keep REPO_ROOT module-level constant AND add `repo_root: Path = REPO_ROOT` kwarg. Alternative considered: drop the module constant entirely and require callers to pass repo_root. Rejected because dashboard_maintainer.py imports `_git_log_oneline_since` and calls it without args — keeping the constant default preserves that import-side behavior unchanged.

For dashboard_maintainer.py's private-method abuse (live._request("POST", ...)): explicitly LEFT for Item 2.6. The 2.4 scope is strictly argparse + ProjectContext threading; chokepoint routing is a separate concern.
